title: Reconstruction source library
description: Bibliographic identity for every source the Angle V2 reconstruction cites (plan section 6.1). Identity lives here exactly once; page/plate/excerpt/interpretation live on each claim reference (claim.schema.json). Cross-field rules (id uniqueness) are enforced by scripts/validate_reconstruction.py.

editionNote.description: Edition, access route, and reliability caveats (e.g. 44-year retrospective; access browser-only; secondary-hop reproduction).

clockProfile.description: Per-source clock-offset assessment (ED-25). Semantics: canonical time = source's stated time + offsetMinutes. Recorded once on the source, inherited by its claims (tier C). Rule 4: report-nominal clocks never define or move an anchor — the profile exists so provenance UI can display the skew. Timestamped dispatches are document pins, not witness clocks, and carry no profile. Cross-field rules (envelope brackets offset; anchor id shape) are enforced by scripts/validate_reconstruction.py.

offsetEnvelope.description: [min, max] honest spread of (chain adopted − source stated) differences, widened to the kind default when fewer than two anchor statements exist (watch-checked ±5; contemporaneous-civilian ±5; retrospective-watch ±15; tablet-adjudicated ±30, ±60 on the McLaws wing; report-nominal ±45).

anchorsUsed.description: Canonical anchors the offset derivation compared against (reproducibility).

assessment.description: Method note: which anchor statements were compared and how.
